hero.title: Smart Pre-Surgery Planning System For Brain
hero.subtitle: Helping doctors make more accurate pre-operative decisions using Artificial Intelligence.

overview.title: Project Overview
overview.intro: Our project is a complete intelligent system for brain pre-surgery planning that leverages MRI data and Artificial Intelligence to segment tumors, analyze critical brain regions, and determine the safest surgical path to reach the tumor.
overview.goal_text: The system aims to assist doctors in selecting the optimal surgical trajectory to reach the tumor while minimizing risk, by analyzing multi-modal MRI scans and understanding the spatial relationship between the tumor and critical functional brain areas.
overview.tech_text: The project utilizes advanced tools such as PyTorch and MONAI for medical image processing, a U-Net model for tumor segmentation, ANTs for accurate 3D image registration, and PyVista for interactive 3D visualization.
overview.impact_text: The system improves surgical accuracy and reduces potential damage to critical brain regions, enhancing patient safety and supporting more confident pre-operative medical decisions.
overview.features_text: Multi-modal MRI analysis (T1, T1ce, T2, FLAIR), tumor segmentation into sub-regions, functional brain area mapping, 3D risk map generation, and optimal surgical path selection based on multiple criteria.
overview.workflow_text: The pipeline starts with MRI data preprocessing and normalization, followed by tumor segmentation using U-Net. Functional brain masks are then generated, combined into a 3D risk map, and finally the A* algorithm is used to compute the safest surgical path based on path length, risk, and trajectory angle.
overview.benefits_text: Model performance is evaluated using Dice Score for overlap accuracy and HD95 for boundary precision. These metrics ensure reliable segmentation and continuous model improvement.
